headline: Subang Airport RM8 Billion Redevelopment Plan Proposed
context: Consortium proposed RM8 billion redevelopment of Sultan Abdul Aziz Shah Airport as premium short-haul hub for 25 million passengers.

cards.big: RM8 billion Subang redevelopment proposed for 25 million passengers
cards.sub: Plan transforms aging airport into premium short-haul and business aviation hub

cards.big: Current facility handles only 2.8 million passengers at 40% original capacity
cards.sub: Firefly and Berjaya Air are sole scheduled operators since AirAsia moved 2014

cards.big: Proposed development includes 3.5km runway extension and new terminal
cards.sub: Surrounding area valued at RM28 billion for largest mixed-use development

cards.big: Residents within 5km oppose expansion citing noise and traffic concerns
cards.sub: Petition with 45,000 signatures submitted to Selangor state government

cards.big: Airport revival tests whether development benefits outweigh community impact
RM8 billion Subang redevelopment — the plan must serve commuters and residents, not just aviation demand.
